key_insight: MEDA combines an LLM agent with symbolic regression to automatically discover biologically meaningful ODE models, not just trajectory-fitting equations.
engineering_relevance: This points to a practical workflow for model discovery in systems biology, where prior knowledge and mechanistic constraints can improve interpretability and reliability of learned dynamics.
method: Retrieves background knowledge and defines admissible state variables
Generates mechanistic constraints and candidate ODE structures
Fits and evaluates models with symbolic regression and numerical optimization
evidence_signal: The system was tested on canonical retrieval, extrapolation to unseen variants, and open-ended discovery, with and without experimental data, and the abstract reports strong structural recovery and biologically plausible models.
watch_item: A key concern is that numerical fitting alone can yield trajectory-matching equations that are biologically incorrect, so constraint quality and knowledge formalization are critical.
paper_title: Automatic Ordinary Differential Equations Discovery For Biological Systems Using Large Language Model Powered Agentic System
